meta.json_run_notes: Delaware has relatively limited standalone whistleblower statutes compared to larger jurisdictions. Primary coverage centers on the Delaware Whistleblowers’ Protection Act and select sector-specific protections. Additional statutes may exist in niche regulatory contexts but were excluded due to insufficient confidence. Batch truncated for quality control to avoid speculative entries.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Prohibits employer retaliation against employees who report violations of law or refuse to participate in illegal activities.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: Derived from general civil limitations period; statute text does not clearly specify.

records._review_notes: Primary private-sector whistleblower protection in Delaware. Covers both reporting and refusal to engage in illegal conduct. Statute of limitations appears derived from general civil law and should be verified.

records.official_name: Delaware False Claims and Reporting Act

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects individuals from retaliation for lawful acts in furtherance of a false claims action.

reward.reward_details: Qui tam relators may receive a percentage of recovered funds.

records._review_notes: State false claims act includes anti-retaliation protections and financial incentives. Applies broadly to fraud against the state. Reviewer should confirm statutory citation formatting and remedies.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects healthcare workers who report unsafe patient care or regulatory violations.

records._review_notes: Applies to healthcare personnel reporting patient safety concerns. Statutory structure and enforcement mechanisms require verification. SOL not clearly identified in statute text.

error_details: Batch truncated for quality control — only 3 of 5 requested records produced due to limited high-confidence statutes in scope
DE-1703 statute of limitations derived from general civil law — marked ambiguous
DE-4004 statute identification and structure require verification — low confidence in citation and scope
No case citations included for well-litigated statutes (e.g., DE-1703) due to inability to verify approved-source URLs
